spec_match.rationale: Wraps each source in a `trapped()` generator that logs every pull, then runs `Iterator.concat(A, B, C).drop().filter().take()` and visualises which cells were touched — a literal trace of the spec's lazy-pull semantics.

interactive.rationale: Live take(n) slider, drop and filter toggles, an eager-spread comparison toggle, three timeline rows with per-cell coloured states (pulled / delivered / never-touched / eager finite pull), and per-run totals — the most pedagogical concept in this folder.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a slide-to-change-N dial showing pulled/delivered/skipped cells — page delivers exactly that with three sources including an infinite C.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers take(0), take large enough to exhaust A+B and dip into C, the filter-out-negatives case, and the drop interaction. Infinite source C is rendered only partially (8 cells) so visitors see the 'never touched' visualization without the page hanging, and the eager-spread overlay marks every finite A/B cell while warning that eagerly spreading C would never finish.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5193275975794688 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables-driven, cell colour swatches use accent-* tokens (pull/consumed/skipped), no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Animate the pull sequence
openQuestions.detail: The current view is end-state-only. Add a 'step through' button that advances the trace one pull at a time, so visitors see the order in which sources are entered (A exhausts, B starts, drop/filter intercept, take stops).

openQuestions.title: Add `flatMap` to the chain
openQuestions.detail: `drop` + `filter` + `take` are great. Add `flatMap` as another optional helper because flatMap is where laziness becomes essential — a flatMap on a finite source could expand to infinite work without it.

summary: Best teaching tool in this feature folder - literally shows the spec's lazy-pull contract on a per-cell timeline, now with an eager-spread overlay that marks every finite A/B cell and explains why the infinite C source would hang if spread eagerly. Verified in Chrome Canary 151 with chrome-devtools-mcp by changing take values, toggling drop/filter/eager, and checking the per-run totals. Step-through animation and flatMap remain as minor follow-ups.
